# Demi

> A ritual for people who half-believe. Demi is a 30-second daily focus ritual that holds your future self in view, so the ordinary Tuesday she lives inside can finally find you. Built by a small, independent studio in San Francisco.

## About

Demi (formally Demimanifest) is an iOS app for daily intention-setting. It is not a religion app, not a crystals app, and not therapy. It is a 30-second attention practice that works the way a workout works — not because you believe in it, but because you keep doing it.

Your goal, streak, and taps live on your device. Nothing leaves unless you explicitly sync. The goal is end-to-end encrypted.

## Core pages

- [Home](https://demimanifest.com/): the manifesto, how it works, the ten rituals, and FAQ
- [About](https://demimanifest.com/about): the team and philosophy
- [Contact](https://demimanifest.com/contact): press, feedback, collaboration
- [Privacy](https://demimanifest.com/privacy): what we collect and why (very little)
- [Terms](https://demimanifest.com/terms): terms of service

## How the ritual works

1. Name your future self — one sentence, one Tuesday
2. Open Demi once a day, for 30 seconds
3. Tap through the ritual — read, breathe, hold it
4. Close the app and go live the day

Thirty seconds. Daily. Two weeks to feel the difference.

## Key principles

- Half-belief is welcome. "Demi-" means half.
- The woo is aesthetic; the practice is just attention, placed on purpose.
- One product, made carefully, on a long timeline. No venture capital.
- Privacy by design: end-to-end encryption, anonymous analytics off by default.
